channel.title: Jira
channel.description: An XML representation of a search request

item.title: [FHIR-54865] Specimen reference to Group seems like overkill
item.description:
Short Display Specimen used for this observation
Cardinality 0..1
Type Reference([Specimen https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/specimen.html] | Group)

 

This seems overly complex, requiring an extra layer of "indirection." What justification is there for modeling it this way vs simply increasing the maximum cardinality to *. 

Propose consulting with a wide range of implementers to validate whether this approach or changing the cardinality is better.
item.summary: Specimen reference to Group seems like overkill

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: This was added in https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-34002

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

item.title: [FHIR-50765] Experimental Logical Models
item.description: There are three logical models marked as experimental.  If they are experimental, why are they being published at this time?  Also, they represent net new FHIR resources - seems like something that should be brought to FHIR-I.  Suggest removing.
item.summary: Experimental Logical Models

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: I like the changes
Ignore my comment - I was looking at the wrong pre-application.

I think the definitions of the new logical models in the preapplication are unclear because they talk about resources making me think they exist in FHIR.  You have:

Environmental Standard PHR profile of the Environmental resource.
Financial Receipt Financial receipt for over-the-counter (OTC) purchases. Based off the Claim resource.
Nutrient Outtake Standard PHR profile of the NutrientOuttake resource.
SocialMedia Standard PHR profile of the SocialMedia resource.

I suggest:

Environmental a Logical Model to represent environmental features such as termperature and humidity.
Financial Receipt a Logical Model representing the receipt from purchase of an over-the-counter (OTC) purchases.  (Based off the Claim resource, but not a profile of the Claim resource)
Nutrient Outtake ?????
SocialMedia a Logical Model representing.....

 

I looked up Nutrient Outtake and can't find anything called that.  Did you mean output or intake?  Also, there are no attributes in Nutrient Outtake or Social Media - strikes me we should just save these for an STU2.
Thinking of moving Logical Models from 'experimental' to 'trial-use'.  But, we will want to create Profiles for them (in the PGHD pull request).
I'm okay moving them up from experimental.  We just need to flesh them out.

item.title: [FHIR-51489] Request for patch on 3.1.1 Organization identifier cardinality
item.description:
In 3.1.1, the CLIA and NPI identifiers were limited to a maximum of 1.  This was corrected in STU 6 to allow unlimited repetitions.

Da Vinci is trying to define an organization profile that is technically conformant with 3.1.1, 6.1.0 and 7.0.0.  The validation tooling is currently complaining about this one discrepancy.  If the cardinality in 3.1.1 was an error and the intention was always to allow for the possibility that organizations might have more than one NPI or CLIA identifier, it would be very helpful if this could be formally declared as a 'patch'.
item.summary: Request for patch on 3.1.1 Organization identifier cardinality

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment:
You add grouping (..*-us-core-patch-pending) to a tracker and ask CGP to review!

We have had approximately 10 patches in 6-years.

Let me know if you have suggestions to improve – see process here: 

 

https://confluence.hl7.org/spaces/CGP/pages/81034169/US+Core+Patch+Process

To clarify, the existing of the 'patch' grouping on a tracker means it's approved as a 3.1.1 patch?

What's the process to request a patch in the future if not submitting a ticket this way?
I probably gave you bad instructions originally to create it.
can you close this tracker since the Patch is applied to the original tracker?
Presume the patch will cover both Organization and Practitioner?
Patch applies to this tracker - https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-39382

from US Core 6.0.0 change log: "Applied: Compatible, substantive (Enhancement) Allow multiple NPIs for Organizations and Practitioners FHIR-39382 See Changes Here"

Review of Patches page

 

Would this Patch accomplish what the commenter requests?

If Patch only affects 3.1.1 new certifiers, has that ship already sailed since certification has moved on to 6/USCDI Version 3.1?   Would this be a new "Certification Enforcement Discretion for USCDI 1"?

item.title: [FHIR-53194] Update the XtEHR mappings in the document
item.description:
Upadte the XtEHR mapping using the QA preview versions: XtEHR version 0.2.1 QA preview has been published on the new canonical https://www.xt-ehr.eu/fhir/models/

 

This should be done for R4 and R5. Remove the copies from the repo, refer to the published version directly.
item.summary: Update the XtEHR mappings in the document

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

item.title: [FHIR-55949] The name used for the Devices workgroup is incorrect
item.description:
FHIR Core is still using the old name "Health Care Devices" for the "Devices" working group. 

This name is used as the "Responsible Owner" for the DeviceMetric and DeviceAlert resources. It is also used on the "by work group" tab on the resource list page.

This issue is related to this issue filed on IG Publisher: https://github.com/HL7/fhir-ig-publisher/issues/1252
item.summary: The name used for the Devices workgroup is incorrect

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

item.title: [FHIR-55735] Narrative Requirements rendering issues
item.description:
Rendering issues for several requirements and their linked locations:

  • §1: spec-77:A subscription-based mechanism SHALL be used by the client to be informed of updates to the authorization.
  • §2: spec-77:A subscription-based mechanism SHALL be used by the client to be informed of updates to the authorization. §1
  • §3: spec-33:Each item returned on the PAS ClaimResponse SHALL echo the same item.sequence as that same item had on the Claim. The item.sequence element SHALL serve as the main tracing identifier of items throughout requests and responses.
  • §4: spec-33:Each item returned on the PAS ClaimResponse SHALL echo the same item.sequence as that same item had on the Claim. The item.sequence element SHALL serve as the main tracing identifier of items throughout requests and responses.
  • §ainfo-1: Payers MAY request additional information in a number of ways (key not rendering in the Requirements resource narrative table)
  • §prof-1: Timings SHALL have at least a count, frequency and period, a calendarPattern, or a deliveryPattern (link image icon strangeness and key not rendering in the Requirements resource narrative table)
  • §prof-2: like prof-1
item.summary: Narrative Requirements rendering issues

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: There is a bug in the rendering of block conformance statements.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will fix the rendering of these conformance requirements.

item.title: [FHIR-55679] Clarify number of OperationOutcome instances in an error response
item.description:
“It is possible that the incoming prior authorization Bundle can not be processed due to validation errors or other non-business-errors. In these instances, the receiving system SHALL return OperationOutcome instances that detail why the Bundle could not be processed and no ClaimResponse will be returned.”

The use of plural for “OperationOutcome instances” is potentially confusing: could you return a Bundle with multiple OperationOutcomes in response? I don’t think that is the intent. Recommend clarifying that this is referring to the error case for an Operation invocation (e.g., 4XX error), in which case a single OperationOutcome is returned instead of the Parameters or unwrapped return parameter value per https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/operations.html#response.

Also update “The resulting Bundle is returned as the HTTP body of the POST response.” to be “The resulting Bundle or OperationOutcome is returned as the HTTP body of the POST response.”
item.summary: Clarify number of OperationOutcome instances in an error response

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

item.title: [FHIR-55674] Clarify which referenced resources are in the Response Bundle
item.description:
The Response section includes the following three statements:

  1. This Claim resource does not need to be included in the Bundle
  2. All other resources that were sent in the Claim request can also be returned as just references in the ClaimResponse.
  3. All resources that are referenced by the ClaimResponse need to be returned in the response Bundle.

The first two appear to be exceptions to the 3rd. Resolve the contradiction with clear language and use conformance verbs like it done for similar requirements around referenced resource inclusion for submission.
item.summary: Clarify which referenced resources are in the Response Bundle

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will fix the contradictions in those statements.

item.title: [FHIR-55672] Clarify Claim reference requirements in ClaimResponse
item.description:
"The ClaimResponse requires that a Claim be referenced in the ClaimResponse.request element. This Claim resource does not need to be included in the Bundle but the Claim URL or identifier does need to be sent so that the ClaimResponse can be matched to the original Claim."

This sentence appears to provide a clarification around and possibly constraints on the ClaimResponse.request element in the PAS ClaimResponse profile. If so, clarify what the constraints are. Specific questions:

  • Am I correct in understanding that the Claim Identifier would be sent in ClaimResponse.request.identifier element?
  • If so, is ClaimResponse.request.identifier required if the Claim is not present? Is that a must support element?
  • What Claim element does “Claim URL” refer to? How would it be included in the ClaimResponse?
item.summary: Clarify Claim reference requirements in ClaimResponse

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will update and clarify the sentence to be clearer.

item.title: [FHIR-55670] Clarify confusing SHALL / SHOULD combination for unique resources in Bundles
item.description:
In the narrative text, §spec-17 and §spec-18 (narrative section), and §spec-36 and §spec-37 (narrative section), are separated by a "e.g.," suggesting that the second is a restatement of the first. However, they are in fact different requirement discussing two different things, the first is an easy to verify requirement about the same id appearing multiple times, while the second is a subjective requirement about when two resources refer to the same real-world entity. Recommend they be restated more clearly such as in the following way:

  • Each unique resource instance by id SHALL appear at most once in the Bundle. (17 and 36)
  • Only one unique resource instance SHOULD be created to represent the same information. In other words, avoid creating multiple Practitioner instances for the same person. (18 and 37)
item.summary: Clarify confusing SHALL / SHOULD combination for unique resources in Bundles

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will fix the highlighted conformance statements.

item.title: [FHIR-55667] Clarify which US Core Condition and Observation Profiles to Support
item.description:
spec-1 says "implementations SHALL also support the US Core R4 profiles for Condition, Observation, and Procedure." However, there are two US Core profiles for Condition (Encounter Diagnosis and Problems and Health Concerns) and many US core profiles on Observations.

Clarify whether it is enough to support a single profile on these resource types, or if all are needed, or otherwise clarify when support for specific profiles is needed.
item.summary: Clarify which US Core Condition and Observation Profiles to Support

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will expand the conformance statement to be clearer about which US Core profiles are needed to support. (When this was written, we believe US Core only had one profile for each).

item.title: [FHIR-51903] Specify which X12 IG contains mappings
item.description:
I'm not sure whether this is in scope for the ballot but in the STU Note it has a link to the Da Vinci External Reference page, and that page doesn't state exactly which X12 specification contains the mappings. Could you update it to be more clear about that? In this case I assume you mean 005010X217, although there are also FHIR mappings in 005010X215.

This section also mentions "the X12 TRN03 guide" but that doesn't make any sense. There is no such X12 implementation guide. TRN03 is a data element used in multiple segments in multiple X12 implementation guides.
item.summary: Specify which X12 IG contains mappings

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: Prior Authorization Implementation Guide Home Page

We will update the STU Note to indicate the specific X12 guides (the 278 request/response and inquiry/response) and to indicate that the mappings have been done in the 0510 version but that future versions may contain updated mappings.

item.title: [FHIR-51954] Patient could be a dependent
item.description:
One of the Modified Content blocks states as follows.

NOTE: The inquiry operation must contain the details of the organization making the query (the X12 Requester, Loop 2000B), a payer organization (the X12 UMO, Loop 2000A), and a patient (Loop 2000C) for the inquiry. The operation does not allow inquiries that do not identify a specific patient, such as an inquiry for all prior authorization requests submitted on a specific date. The patient will be identified by their member identifier. This identifier is a specific slice on the Patient resource that is identified with a type of 'MB'. The identifier system will indicate the payer that the member identifier is associated with.

This isn't always correct because the patient could be the subscriber in Loop 2000C or a dependent in Loop 2000D. If the patient is a dependent then they can't be identified by their member identifier. This gets confusing but in the context of X12 transactions a patient is only classified as a dependent if they don't have their own unique member ID, regardless of whether the health plan internally considers them to be a dependent of a subscriber. I'm not certain but handling this properly might require some changes to the inquiry profile.
item.summary: Patient could be a dependent

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: https://github.com/HL7/davinci-pas/commit/14922014b0d351c8e22b5453dda2a2a417bb2f31

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
We will modify that paragraph to say:

"and a patient (Loop 2000C) for the inquiry. As per the X12 Inquiry guide, loop 2000C is always valued when inquiring about authorizations for a specific patient.  Loop 2000D is also used when necessary to identify a patient as a dependent.  Thus Loop 2000D may be present in the case where the patient is a dependent."

item.title: [FHIR-51958] Discrepancy in CMS Place of Service code format
item.description: For the CMS Location codes it looks like we have copied from the CMS Place of Service Code Set, however the leading zeros were dropped from the one-digit codes. The PoS codes are always sent as two characters in administrative transactions so if we change the format that will confuse implementers and causing matching failures. For example, the code for Pharmacy should be "01", not "1".
item.summary: Discrepancy in CMS Place of Service code format

status.description: The change request has been identified as a duplicate of an existing change request. No further action will be taken on this change request

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: CMS Location Codes Value Set;CRD Temporary Codes

item.title: [FHIR-55932] Remove complies with US Core 3.1.1
item.description:
US Core 3.1.1 is no longer required in regulation (it has been expired) and has an restriction on Organization.identifier[CLIA] 0..1 which was later relaxed to 0..* in 6.1, which causes a compatibility issue (unless we also restrict it in CARIN BB. Since the regulator requirement has been expired, there is no need for this specific restriction. Old data will still work with the new guide.

 

Remove the dependency on US Core 3.1.1 and the complies with to Patient, Practitioner, Organization, Coverage, and RelatedPerson profiles. Also make the necessary changes to the Background page. (which sites the versions of US Core it build on.
item.summary: Remove complies with US Core 3.1.1

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

customfield.customfieldvalues: This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: C4BB Coverage;C4BB Organization;C4BB Patient;C4BB Practitioner;C4BB RelatedPerson

Change the base inheritance to US Core 7.0.0 and remove complies with US Core 3.1.1.

(US Core 8.0.1 introduces incompatible changes.)

item.title: [FHIR-55907] add/clarify some CPT related items
item.description:
Clarify CPT vs HCPCS semantics and “round-tripping” by adding--

Add explicit guidance (and examples) for round-trip preservation:
If a client sends CPT, how to preserve the original CPT representation while still meeting PAS/X12 normalization behavior (i.e., avoid losing clinician-facing CPT context).

Recommend a consistent strategy for “original code” vs “normalized code” (could be documented without changing core profiles, or via an optional extension pattern if Da Vinci wants one).

Tighten examples around service-type coding by adding--

Provide a set of PAS examples where:
“Requested service” is expressed with CPT (professional service) and/or HCPCS II (DME/drugs)

The corresponding CRD/DTR handoff remains consistent (same conceptual service, different representations)
item.summary: add/clarify some CPT related items

status.description: The proposal will not be considered as part of the current release. It will be re-activated and reviewed as part of the next release cycle

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will consider adding examples and guidance in a future version of the specification as this is too late to add at this time to 2.2.0

item.title: [FHIR-55275] ServiceRequest.intent has no escape valve
item.description:
The element ServiceRequest.intent is a mandatory element with a required binding, but appears to have neither an 'unknown' or 'other' concept, which can make the containing element impossible to populate in some cases. Please add 'unknown', 'other', or some equivalent escape valve concept to the value set.

(Comment 239 - imported by: Lloyd McKenzie)
item.summary: ServiceRequest.intent has no escape valve

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: Motion to reopen based on technical challenges applying: David / Rob 6 - 0 - 0 **

Reverted previous resolution: Persuasive with Modification made 2026-02-20 00:00:00.0 with vote Rob Hausam / Yanick Gaudet : 5 - 0 - 0//(Impact: Compatible, substantive; Category: Enhancement; Version:

{"keys":["R6"]}

)//We will:

1. Relax the cardinality for .intent to 0..1

2. Change the binding for .intent to extensible 

2. Request FHIR-I consider a similar change in the request pattern for .intent
The request isn't to change the binding strength, it's to extend the value set to provide an escape valve within the bound value set
.intent is a code element which cannot be made extensible
We will vote to re-open on 2/24 OO on the FHIR call if we have a quorum. A concern has been raised about the cardinality change.

At 2026-02-12 OO main call it decided that as the valueset and codesystem do not belong to OO we should complete our review as with other tickets, note our recommendation, and then re-assign the ticket to the appropriate work group. In this case the valueset is owned by FHIR-I and the codesystem by CDS. Consensus was to send these to FHIR-I if any change is recommended.
Related tickets: https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55262 and https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55267

ServiceRequest.intent is ultimately bound to a code system that is not owned by OO and spans multiple resources: https://build.fhir.org/codesystem-request-intent.html.

The code system does not support the other/unknown escape valve concept.

Further work would be required here.

item.title: [FHIR-55954] Update grammar and tone
item.description:
Update grammar and tone of 

There is a basic need to access records in your language,...

to 

There is a need for patients to access their records in their preferred language,...
item.summary: Update grammar and tone

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

item.title: [FHIR-55906] add an example set
item.description:
In the terminology section add a normative/implemented example set showing:
A ServiceRequest containing a clinical code + a CPT “likely billing” repetition (where known) This would be good to capture all codes involved.
item.summary: add an example set

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment: Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb 18 BR call

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
We already have an example.  See here: https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/davinci-crd/en/ServiceRequest-example.json.html

 

The standard 'narrative' rendering on the main page of the example doesn't show custom extensions like this, but they're there in the JSON/XML.

item.title: [FHIR-55943] Group Profile type element is too restricted
item.description:
It may be overly restrictive to require Group.type to be fixed to practitioner, especially since we are substituting it with CareTeam. Our intent is to represent CareTeams within the directory to support non–patient-focused care coordination teams, particularly those needed for CMS initiatives such as Accountable Organizations, Pay for performance. 

Look at R5 group.type, it has expended to include codes such as  careteam, healthcareservice, organization.  Should we take those codes into consideration.
item.summary: Group Profile type element is too restricted

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: National Directory of Healthcare Providers and Services (NDH) (FHIR)

item.title: [FHIR-55940] Display name of "patient" doc-needed value is "Administrative & clinical doc"
item.description:
The value set for doc-needed includes the code patient, which has a long-form description reading:

Details most likely to originate from the patient or their personal representative (e.g. parent, spouse, etc.) are required to satisfy additional documentation requirements, determine coverage and/or prior auth applicability. For example, information about household composition, accessibility considerations, etc. This should be used when the data needs to come from the patient themselves, rather than a clinician's assessment of the patient

However, the display name is "Administrative & clinical doc", which does not match the definition of the code, and seems to be a hold-over from when there was a both code to represent administrative in addition to clinical documentation in STU 2.0.
item.summary: Display name of "patient" doc-needed value is "Administrative & clinical doc"

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 966bf7e

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: CRD Coverage Information Additional Documentation Value Set

item.title: [FHIR-55582] CMS Location Codes Value Set doesn't seem to have all the changes described in FHIR-52059
item.description:
See https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-52059

Per FHIR-52059 – 66 should be added but also says, “However, 32, 33, 41, and 42 are deliberately omitted.  They are already covered by other codes in the value set and therefore can't be present.”  I see 66 was added, but 32, 33, 41, and 42 are found, not omitted

related issue – on the https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/davinci-crd/en/history.html#change-history page, the system link next to the entry for FHIR-52056 goes to the CRD temp codes not the CMS codes (https://build.fhir.org/ig/HL7/davinci-crd/en/ValueSet-cmslocationcodes.html).

 

Related to FHIR-55567 - same issue with link to temp code system not cms location code valueset linked from the reference to FHIR-52057 in the Change Log.
item.summary: CMS Location Codes Value Set doesn't seem to have all the changes described in FHIR-52059

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Git master 6ddc094
Reviewed and accepted proposed disposition on Feb 18 BR call

This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
The changes agreed to in FHIR-52059 were superseded by other changes - specifically the fact that there's now an official URL for CMS codes (so no need for temporary codes) and because we're no longer using the CMS codes to supplement, but instead saying to send all CMS codes alongside the official code.

Will update the change log to reflect that this change was superseded by FHIR-53648

item.title: [FHIR-54399] Add a means to identify original appointment in waitlisted appointment
item.description: For waitlisted appointments, there should be a way to identify an appointment to replace in case this one occurs. There is a replaces element, that could be used for this, but there is no guidance about it being used for this purpose. In fact the description says it is replacing a cancelled appointment. Some guidance on how to do specify this link should be added so that if the waitlisted appointment is accepted, the original (less desirable) appointment can be cancelled or somehow flagged for manual follow-up.
item.summary: Add a means to identify original appointment in waitlisted appointment

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.
This is default title:This is default message and can be changed from field configuration.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Cooper Thompson 2/24/2026 2:57 PM

TODO:  finish drafting language below.  See other TODOs at the bottom

--------------------

Update the workflow table to update the Appointment.replaces attribute.

Additionally, update the Appointment.replaces definition to include verbiage about it being used for a potentially cancelled appointment due to a waitlist appointment being created.

Add another workflow table for the use case of a stand-alone waitlist appointment in cases where an appointment can't originally be booked first.

 

-------DRAFT -----------------

Waitlisting the appointment (optional):

This optional step permits creating a waitlisted appointment. This can occur when either no appropriate slot is available, or a time that is not ideal for the patient.

If there is no available slot, then simply create the appointment with a status of waitlist, and optionally include a requestedPeriod. This can happen for services that have limited supply and high demand as they often have long waitlists. Allocation of slots to these type of appointments is often processed based on a triage process that considers various factors.

Alternately a booking can be scheduled and also waitlist a second appointment with a requestedPeriod spanning the time that the patient would prefer if new slots become available
(linking to the booked appointment using the replaces property on the waitlisted appointment).
e.g. Book an available appointment that is 4 months out, but request a waitlist item to get in earlier if a slot becomes available (due to someone else cancelling).

If new time slots become available during the requestedPeriod, the scheduling system, or staff at the scheduling organization, can notify the patient that a new time slot is available. If the patient chooses, the waitlisted appointment would then be booked into that specific slot, and the previously booked appointment would be canceled. The specific business process for notifying patients of new availability is not specified, and is up to the implementing system to determine. These slots could be before or after the booked appointment, however could be profiled to restrict.

-------DRAFT -----------------

ToDos:

  • Update the Flow for a patient waitlist to include the replaces property usage
  • Update the Appointment.replaces property to include notes to describe that it can be used for waitlisted appointments, where the linked appointment "might" cancel, but hasn't been yet.
  • Determine if we want to use replaces for two meanings:  1) one appointment has actually replaced another appointment (resolved waitlist) and 2) one appointment is a potential replacement for another appointment (unresolved waitlist)

item.summary: test

item.title: [FHIR-55339] Potentially re-useable THO-candidate value sets in resource Composition
item.description:
The Composition references several value sets that are potential candidates for migration to THO. While value set migration is not required, if it's realistic for the value set definition to need to change in the coming years, or the value sets might be useful in CDA, V2 or other specs, migration should be considered. Specifics: CodeSystem: composition-attestation-mode (CompositionAttestationMode) referenced by ValueSet composition-attestation-mode bound to element Composition.attester.mode;

(Comment 303 - imported by: Lloyd McKenzie)
item.summary: Potentially re-useable THO-candidate value sets in resource Composition

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: The ValueSet/composition-attestation-mode (https://build.fhir.org/valueset-composition-attestation-mode.html) should move into THO
marc.duteau would you be willing to help me assess Composition value sets that are appropriate to move to THO and begin the process to move those that are?

The composition-attestation-mode code system looks like it should move to THO unless TSMG has given an exemption

For the value sets, there are two questions:

  • Could they potentially be used in a different product family
  • Is there a chance implementers might need them updated sooner than the next release of FHIR (or even need changes that would be effective in this version of FHIR).  This is particular relevant for those with tight bindings, such as ArtifactRelationshipType, though having extensible bindings that change is also challenging.

In the end, it's up to the work group to make the decision.  The ballot feedback was part of an automated process to flag artifacts that were at least candidates to move and ensure that work groups made conscious choices about what should live where.
Please provide the list of sets you feel are reusable across other families or resources

We will move 

The ValueSet/composition-attestation-mode (https://build.fhir.org/valueset-composition-attestation-mode.html) into THO
